km^3/s^2) in ECI/GCRF; coast arcs are Keplerian; burns are instantaneous (the provided 'acceleration' vectors are treated as Δv impulses in km/s).\n\nMethod:\n1) Converted each provided state to an orbit and used a Δv cost heuristic (altitude-change + plane-change with split-plane-change Hohmann, plus a bi-elliptic option for large plane changes) and exact DP (Held–Karp) to choose the visit order.\n2) For each leg, solved an exact Lambert rendezvous (two-impulse) between the departure orbit state at t_depart and the target station state at t_arrive, choosing the lower-Δv short/long-way solution over a search of departure times and flight times.  \n3) One leg (Station 7 → Station 13) uses a 3-burn high-apoapsis bi-elliptic plane-change to reduce Δv versus any 2-burn solution;
